purpose: Re-run the active Complex architecture through the implementation-grounded external transfer standard without pretending that upstream success validates Complex.

evidence_claim_vocabulary.illustrates: A filled example shows the intended shape.
evidence_claim_vocabulary.screens: A marker or checker catches a bounded structural failure.
evidence_claim_vocabulary.reproduces: A pinned upstream mechanism ran locally.
evidence_claim_vocabulary.compares: Baseline and candidate ran on the same locked case and scorer.
evidence_claim_vocabulary.validates: Repeated real Complex outcomes improved in the stated scope.

rejected_patterns: One standalone mechanism for every recent failure.
Fixed beat counts or self-reported completion.
Making a workflow engine, issue tracker, or coding benchmark mandatory.
object_rebaselines.not_transferable: Complex must remain usable as a small Codex skill and repository runtime kit without a daemon, database, or Python workflow engine.
repository_changes: Merged prompt rehydration, cadence, source resolution, target-function Loop, forward-artifact acceptance, interrupt-resume, and four-layer alignment into the kernel or responsibility boundary.
Removed standalone validated identities until structured same-task outcome evidence exists.
object_rebaselines.next_validation: The redacted-real comparison and writable continuation comparison both reached equal outcomes. Run repeated App or instrumented-surface samples that expose Goal lifecycle and route execution, then obtain independent human review.
provisional_micro_contracts: One runtime kernel owns state restoration, execution, observation, routing, and stop semantics.
Platform primitives and Complex conventions are named separately.
Long-run continuation uses durable state, explicit checkpoints, bounded retries, and observable completion predicates.

rejected_patterns: A second manually maintained copy of protocol prose in the explainer site.
Beginner documentation that exposes raw app-server schemas or candidate mechanism names.
Hard-coded volatile inventory counts when they can be derived.
object_rebaselines.not_transferable: Kubernetes localization, Hugo, release-branch, and SIG review machinery are unnecessary for this repository.
repository_changes: Reframed public pages around Start, Understand, Evaluate, Reference, and Contribute.
Replaced validation overclaims with bounded evidence verbs.
object_rebaselines.next_validation: Have a fresh user or clean agent install, locate the skill, start a target project, and explain the four public document types within five minutes.
provisional_micro_contracts: README orients, quickstart guides a task, core describes normative behavior, and external-methods explains design rationale.
Target onboarding never imports Complex maintainer state.

rejected_patterns: Community-consensus machinery that does not fit a small maintained runtime.
object_rebaselines.not_transferable: Rust team authority, final-comment periods, and language-wide archival requirements do not apply.
repository_changes: Made architecture re-baseline an explicit substantial-change packet.
Added compatibility and rollback to future mechanism-level change requirements.
Kept routine corrections on the normal edit/test path.
object_rebaselines.next_validation: Use the packet on the next substantial schema or public-term change and compare review defects and rework with this re-baseline.
provisional_micro_contracts: Only substantial changes require a compact decision packet.
The packet records problem evidence, design, drawbacks, alternatives, compatibility, evaluation, rollback, owner, and status.
Acceptance authorizes an experiment or transfer; it does not validate the outcome.

rejected_patterns: Installing a full evaluation framework as a Complex dependency.
Committing raw private transcripts to the active tree.
object_rebaselines.not_transferable: Complex must support non-Python and non-model-API projects, so plain JSON records and external cold pointers remain sufficient.
repository_changes: Demoted both previously validated mechanisms to tested pending locked comparisons.
Made evidence claim vocabulary mechanically reviewable.
object_rebaselines.next_validation: Convert one redacted real feedback sample into a locked baseline/candidate pair and ask a second reviewer to reproduce the maturity decision.
provisional_micro_contracts: Separate eval_case, eval_run, and score_record identities.
Lock case version, samples, scorer, limits, and one changed variable for a comparison.
Keep marker screens separate from human outcome review and permit re-scoring without rewriting the run.

mechanism_rebaselines.rationale: One kernel is the lowest-friction home for universal runtime behavior.
Authority and side-effect ownership are universal; anti-intervention and resume safety are facets of this boundary.
mechanism_rebaselines.next_validation: Measure over-asking and under-asking on two real project classes.

mechanism_rebaselines.rationale: Every run needs an audience and evidence-strength contract.
mechanism_rebaselines.next_validation: Reader task completion and correction test.

mechanism_rebaselines.rationale: It is a distinct platform adapter, but Complex conventions must not be called Codex primitives.
mechanism_rebaselines.next_validation: Same prompt on two Codex surfaces.

mechanism_rebaselines.rationale: Different project natures require different evaluation and search weights; current external grounding remains partial.
Evaluator independence is distinct from ordinary context management.
Keep marker screening, but move maturity claims to versioned run and score records.
mechanism_rebaselines.next_validation: One redacted baseline/candidate record with second-reviewer reproduction.

mechanism_rebaselines.rationale: Keep as a conditional multi-lane extension and remove universal core wording.
Useful for multi-module projects, excessive as a default for ordinary work.
mechanism_rebaselines.next_validation: Shadow controller on one real portfolio.

mechanism_rebaselines.rationale: Keep only when measured context pressure exists; remove universal numeric thresholds.
mechanism_rebaselines.next_validation: Long-project paired recovery and context-load run.

mechanism_rebaselines.rationale: It is a promotion and transfer discipline, not proof that any current transfer improved Complex.
mechanism_rebaselines.next_validation: Complete two locked same-task transfers with real outcome evidence.

consolidations.reason: Prompt contract and per-beat context assembly already own this behavior.
Cadence is Progress Loop continuation plus Harness heartbeat, not a standalone validated mechanism.
Authorization belongs to the boundary; checkpoint/idempotency belongs to the kernel harness.
It is the inverse necessity test of the same authority boundary.
It duplicates kernel behavior five and the Progress Loop layer.
Forward artifact is one outcome predicate, not an independent mechanism.
Basis/inference/unsupported-claim separation is calibration and delivery evaluation.
Thin closure is execution routing; minimum observability is delivery.
Example currentness is repository evaluation QA, not runtime behavior.
Skill discovery belongs to Harness and target-anchor resolution belongs to Context.
The four layers are diagnostic axes intrinsic to the kernel, not a separate validated identity.
